model_narrative: We are planning travel across a set of cities connected by undirected links, each with a known distance. For each link we decide whether or not to use it. The goal is to choose the links so that the total distance of all the used links is as small as possible.

sets.name: I
sets.doc: the cities that must be visited

sets.name: E
sets.doc: the undirected connections between cities, one member per pair of cities that can be linked; each member is an ordered pair of cities that names a single undirected link, and every link appears exactly once

params.name: d
params.doc: the travel distance of each link between two cities, in distance units

vars.name: x
vars.doc: 1 if the link between the two cities is used, 0 otherwise; one such choice per undirected link

objective.expr_var: total distance of the used links

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Every city must connect to exactly two of its links. For each city, count the used links that touch it, and require that count to equal two.

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Generate the complete constraint set for this model.

query: There's a proposal on the table to lean less on the short hops between nearby stops. We're looking at allowing at most half as many short-range links as there are stops in the network. What does the total routing length come out to under that limit?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_short_links = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[i, j] for (i, j) in model.E if abs(int(i[1:]) - int(j[1:])) <= 2) <= len(model.I) // 2)

query: We're evaluating a routing rule that ties two of stop 4's connections together: whenever stop 4 is linked to stop 3, it should also stay linked to stop 1. Add that dependency and let me see the resulting routing length.

query: There's a proposal to avoid running both the stop 1-to-2 link and the stop 3-to-4 link in the same plan, since they overlap operationally. We're looking at allowing at most one of those two links. How does the total length shift if we add that restriction?

constraints.name: x[i,

query: There's a proposal on the table to spread the routing out so no stop hugs only its immediate neighbours. We're looking at a rule that every stop may keep at most one link to a nearby stop, forcing each one to reach further out. What does that do to the total routing length?

model_narrative: We are planning travel across a set of cities connected by undirected links, each with a known distance. For each link we decide whether or not to use it. The goal is to choose the links so that the total distance of all the used links is as small as possible.

sets.name: I
sets.doc: the cities that must be visited

sets.name: E
sets.doc: the undirected connections between cities, one member per pair of cities that can be linked; each member is an ordered pair of cities that names a single undirected link, and every link appears exactly once

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Generate the complete constraint set for this model.

query: We're looking at a two-part packaging to push the routing toward longer reaches: cap the links between back-to-back stops at half the number of stops, and on top of that allow at most one link that skips exactly one stop. Apply both together and let me see the new total length.
expected_pyomo:
model.cap_consecutive = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[i, j] for (i, j) in model.E if abs(int(i[1:]) - int(j[1:])) <= 1) <= len(model.I) // 2)
model.cap_one_gap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[i, j] for (i, j) in model.E if abs(int(i[1:]) - int(j[1:])) == 2) <= 1)
